## Summary

Hardened `.github/pull_request_template.md` from a minimal chooser stub into a universal fallback template that includes every validator-required section header and key declaration line used by `latency-pr-enforcement`.

This prevents recurring CI failures caused by missing sections when authors open PRs without selecting a typed template.

## Scope

- Updated only: `.github/pull_request_template.md`
- Not touched: workflow logic, classifier rules, branch protection, evaluation runtime paths.

## CI/Infra Scope

This changes PR authoring ergonomics only: new PR descriptions now start with a compliance-complete skeleton by default.

## Rollback Plan

Revert this single file to the prior chooser-only template if needed.

## Affected Workflows

- `.github/workflows/latency-pr-enforcement.yml` — behavior unchanged; this PR only improves compatibility of default PR bodies with existing checks.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

No new input source is introduced. Inputs remain standard PR body text authored by contributors.

## Internal Process Leakage

No internal-only process details are exposed. The template references existing governance sections already required by current enforcement.

## Input → Action → Output

- **Input:** contributor opens PR using default template.
- **Action:** default body already includes required section headings and mandatory declaration lines.
- **Output:** recurring “missing section” CI failures are eliminated; contributors focus on filling content quality rather than discovering missing headings.

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

No new public metrics are introduced.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

None — documentation/template-only change; no runtime or pipeline expansion.

## Latency Impact

None — template-only change with no runtime execution-path impact.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

Primary risk is reduced strictness if contributors leave generic fallback text unchanged. Mitigation: typed templates still recommended, and quality checks still enforce non-empty/non-placeholder sections.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — this PR does not modify evaluation runtime/pipeline code paths.
